pr.title: fix(skills): add normalize_skill_files() helper and document path naming convention
pr.body:
## Summary

Closes #1575.

`beta.skills.create(files=...)` silently requires every path to be prefixed with
a top-level directory whose name **exactly matches** the `name:` field in `SKILL.md`
frontmatter (e.g. `my-skill/SKILL.md`, `my-skill/scripts/tool.py`).  When callers
pass bare names like `SKILL.md` they receive an opaque API 400 with no guidance on
what went wrong.

This PR adds a `normalize_skill_files()` helper in `anthropic.lib.tools` that:

- Reads the `name:` field from the `SKILL.md` entry in the supplied file list
- Re-prefixes every path to `<name>/…`, stripping any existing wrong prefix
- Is a no-op when paths are already correct

It also updates the `files=` param docstring in the generated `beta.skills` resource
to explain the naming convention and cross-reference the helper.

## Changes

- `src/anthropic/lib/tools/_skills.py` — new `_read_file_entry_content`,
  `_parse_skill_name_from_frontmatter`, and `normalize_skill_files` functions
- `src/anthropic/lib/tools/__init__.py` — re-export `normalize_skill_files`
- `src/anthropic/resources/beta/skills/skills.py` — improved `files=` docstring
- `tests/lib/tools/test_skills.py` — 7 new unit tests (all passing)

## Test plan

- [x] `python -m pytest tests/lib/tools/test_skills.py -v` → 19 passed
- [x] Bare names → correctly prefixed with skill name
- [x] Wrong top-level prefix → replaced with correct skill name
- [x] Already-correct prefix → left unchanged
- [x] Extra tuple fields (headers) → preserved
- [x] Missing `SKILL.md` → `ValueError`
- [x] Missing `name:` frontmatter field → `ValueError`
- [x] `BytesIO` content → read and seeked back
pr.author: ppradyoth
